title: theBalancedBriefNLtech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: New York Bans Government Employees from Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
items.description: A new executive order seen by WIRED prohibits New York state employees from using insider knowledge to enrich themselves with prediction market bets.

items.title: USAID Whistleblower Says It Was Even Worse Than People Knew
items.description: Political appointees wanted a quiet drawdown, the whistleblower says. DOGE wanted an execution.

items.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
items.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build...

items.title: AI Tools Are Helping Mediocre North Korean Hackers Steal Millions
items.description: One group of hackers used AI for everything from vibe coding their malware to creating fake company websites—and stole as much as $12 million in three...

items.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
items.description: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

items.title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
items.description: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee"...

items.title: Apple fixes bug that cops used to extract deleted chat messages from iPhones
items.description: The iPhone and iPad bug allowed law enforcement using forensic tools to read messages that had long been deleted by the Signal app.

items.title: 5 AI Models Tried to Scam Me. Some of Them Were Scary Good
items.description: The cyber capabilities of AI models have experts rattled. AI’s social skills may be just as dangerous.

items.title: Best Chromebooks (2026): Most Are Bad, but These Aren't
items.description: Chromebooks have a reputation for being cheap and crappy. But they don't have to be, and these are the Chromebooks that prove it.

items.title: France confirms data breach at government agency that manages citizens’ IDs
